expectations.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
expectations.evidence: No round structure — output is a single linear recommendation document

expectations.text: Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: No personas — single authorial voice throughout

expectations.text: Round 1 includes a thesis, rationale with 3-5 arguments, timeline, resources needed, and upside scenario
expectations.evidence: No Round 1 — document is structured as problem/recommendation, not adversarial debate

expectations.text: Round 2 contains numbered challenges with severity ratings (critical/significant/minor)
expectations.evidence: No Round 2, no severity ratings on challenges

expectations.text: Round 3 addresses each numbered challenge from Round 2, with concessions or counters
Round 4 contains a verdict that is one of: proceed, modify-and-proceed, defer, reject
expectations.evidence: Recommendation is 'No. Do not pivot' — not using the formal verdict vocabulary

expectations.text: Round 4 includes numbered action items with who/what/when
expectations.evidence: Has a phased plan but no numbered action items with specific owners and dates

expectations.text: Debate metrics table with challengesRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesDefeated, challengesConceded, confidenceScore
Round 4 includes a risks accepted section referencing specific challenges
expectations.evidence: Has a risk table but not in the context of accepted debate challenges

expectations.text: Round 4 includes a review date for revisiting the decision
